next_step_text: Confidence 10% June 8th - Follow up demo scheduled Thurs 11th. June 4th - Positive, but short discovery call held, early stage exploration, will require an RFP in due course (no compelling event, likely Q4) next step is to arrange another session to capture more info on the project, provide a conceptual overview for the stakeholders then run a live demo, date TBC w/c 8th Jun. SITUATION ASYAD Group

Build an org chart and map all the key contacts
Prospect's gone cold ? No inbound since last 14 days
10 days without email outbound. Send follow-ups

our_open_deliverables.text: Share meeting slides and Hapag Lloyd case study to Jamila Al Bukhaly

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the strongest current threat as the incumbent RFQ/tender tool and beneficiary of SAP S/4HANA ERP consolidation momentum. Buyer is mid-ERP rollout, creating internal political pressure toward 'one vendor, one support, one integration' even if Ariba is functionally inferior. Jamila stated on 4 Jun that Ariba is under review for replacement due to supplier charges and usability, but if the SAP program lead or CFO prefers ecosystem consolidation, her operational case may be overruled. Coupa, GEP, and iValua are listed in Competitors__c but not mentioned on any call or activity; threat level unknown. Wedge: Zycus offers superior SAP integration (pre-built connectors, faster/cheaper than Ariba), no supplier charges (vs Ariba friction), modern UI driving adoption, and Merlin AI for autonomous sourcing—position as best-of-breed that integrates better with SAP than Ariba does.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Asyad is mid-SAP-S/4HANA-ERP rollout. Internal political momentum toward SAP ecosystem consolidation (one vendor, one support, one integration) even if Ariba functionally inferior. Jamila indicated Ariba under review for replacement due to supplier charges and usability.
competitors.source: MEDDPICC 2.0 CRM entry 8 Jun 2026, Avoma discovery call 4 Jun 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as best-of-breed with superior SAP integration (pre-built connectors, faster/cheaper) vs Ariba rigidity; no supplier charges (removes buyer friction); modern UI driving adoption; make Merlin AI the Phase 2 autonomous sourcing differentiator.

champion_strength.source: MEDDPICC 2.0 CRM entry 8 Jun 2026, Avoma discovery call 4 Jun 2026, Avoma touchpoint manifest 11 Jun 2026
champion_strength.summary: Jamila Al Bukhaly is a developing champion with operational drive and EB access but unproven ability to navigate internal politics. She initiated the engagement after Gartner, articulated pain and criteria clearly, brought Sameh to the demo, and owns the RFP process. However, she is the sole buyer contact (single-thread), and in a $300k group-level decision at a government holding company mid-SAP-ERP-rollout, C-level buy-in and SAP program lead alignment are required. If the CFO or SAP program lead prefers SAP consolidation, her operational case may be insufficient. Champion strength is adequate for current stage (discovery/demo) but must be elevated to executive sponsorship to navigate RFP and decision authority.

sources: Avoma touchpoint manifest 11 Jun 2026 (Sameh attended demo)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Sameh Bartok, SVP Contracts & Procurement, is the economic buyer. MEDDPICC 2.0 states 'He attended the call, confirmed strategic direction, and holds executive authority. Any system procurement requires his sanction.' He attended the 11 Jun demo (touchpoint manifest). Budget is currently assigned to the SAP S/4HANA ERP rollout; S2P is under review within that envelope. Sameh is the likely budget authority but has not yet explicitly confirmed $300k spend approval. His engagement is passive (attended one demo, no active sponsorship signals). In a $300k group-level platform decision at a government holding company, this level of EB engagement is appropriate for early evaluation, but active sign-off and ROI validation are still required before RFP sanction.

recommended_moves: Validate Oman data hosting feasibility with Zycus infrastructure and legal teams and confirm in writing to Jamila and Sameh
Map and engage CFO, CIO, and SAP S/4HANA program lead via Jamila introduction to surface SAP consolidation appetite
Co-author quantified ROI/TCO business case with Jamila showing baseline spend, process cost, and 3-year savings
